message:
fix(core): fix stale memdb reads in LIKE count fast path + ad-hoc DML path

Two bugs combined to make LIKE count queries return 0 after INSERT:

1. execute_table_program_with_cx (ad-hoc conn.execute() DML path) did not
   set memdb_requires_active_txn_reload after VDBE writes. This meant
   COMMIT skipped the MemDatabase reload entirely, leaving it with empty
   tables while the pager had the correct data.

2. SimpleCountLikePattern fast path lacked the memdb_storage_count_shortcuts_safe
   guard that other fast paths (SimpleCountStar, SimpleRowidRangeScan, etc.)
   have. Without this check, it read from stale memdb even when the flag
   indicated memdb rows were unreliable.

Fix A: In execute_table_program_with_cx, set memdb_requires_active_txn_reload
and memdb_rows_loaded=false when VDBE DML invalidates memdb count shortcuts.

Fix B: Add memdb_storage_count_shortcuts_safe check to SimpleCountLikePattern
fast path, matching the pattern used by all other memdb-reading fast paths.

This fixes the string/pattern matching benchmark showing ~130ns for all
table sizes (was reading empty memdb instead of scanning actual rows).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
